slides.title: Windowless Cumulative SACK Extension for RDMA Retransmission
Testing Congestion Control and Queue Management Mechanisms - IETF 126 Hackathon Update
Using a Little’s-Law-Based Queueing-Theoretic Approach for Congestion-Window Updates
Mooncake - Disaggregated Architecture for LLM Inference
[New]Managing Congestion Control Heterogeneity on the Internet with Approximate Performance Isolation

chat.author: Reese Enghardt
chat.text: @Meetecho Camera on speaker, please

chat.author: Alan Jowett
chat.text: 244us is approximately the reciprocal of 4096

chat.author: Alan Jowett
chat.text: so, 4096 times per second == 244us

chat.author: Michael Welzl
chat.text:
Yes - probably:
1) less than 1ms, to avoid the bursts for L4S as Christian said
2) why not 1/4, for example;
3) let's implement 1/4 efficiently in the kernel.
That makes sense to me.

chat.author: Christian Huitema
chat.text: The I in PIE is for integral, ie., assessing the likely impact of past feedback on the various flows. That requires guessing both the RTT of the flows and the response mode of these flows. I wonder what happens if these guesses are wrong.

chat.author: Mohit Tahiliani
chat.text: Yes, but the integral component accumulates error over time rather than instantaneously, so minor RTT or response mode errors might not impact largely. There is an auto-tuning feature as well in RFC 8033 (PIE) that automatically scales the controller parameters depending on the drop probability.

chat.author: Christian Huitema
chat.text: Might not => maybe measure that?

chat.author: Mohit Tahiliani
chat.text: Yes, we have tested it in varying RTT scenarios, and with different response modes.

chat.author: Christian Huitema
chat.text: Did you publish the results?

chat.author: Mohit Tahiliani
chat.text: Yes, I'll share the documents with you.

chat.author: Chris Box
chat.text: Which BBR?

chat.author: Christian Huitema
chat.text: Thanks, Mohit.

chat.author: Chris Box
chat.text: I'm guessing 2019 means version 1

chat.author: Ike Kunze
chat.text: @Chris: yes, that paper was about version 1

chat.author: Chris Box
chat.text: thx

chat.author: Michael Welzl
chat.text: This (performance isolation) sounds like a system that could give people all we need from L4S, simpler.

chat.author: Christian Huitema
chat.text: You mean DualQ AQM? Yes, this presentation seems more interesting.

chat.author: Christian Huitema
chat.text: Also, without using equations hat used to model TCP 30 years ago....

chat.author: Michael Welzl
chat.text: Yes. The whole instantaneous marking, Prague reaction part of L4S is only relevant for the many (irony alert) delay-sensitive but also queue-building flows that exist...

chat.author: Chris Box
chat.text: Is "most of the internet" using FIFO? Not sure that is true of access network bottlenecks.

chat.author: Mohit Tahiliani
chat.text: Flow Queuing  separates old and new flows, and also prioritize the ones that are sparse and do not have a standing queue

chat.author: Michael Welzl
chat.text: Yeah but this proposed system scales better.

chat.author: Christian Huitema
chat.text: I would be happy with two queues, one for CCA that don't do bufferbloat and one for those that do...

chat.author: Michael Welzl
chat.text: I mean, I agree, not "+1" in: I suggest 3  :-D

chat.author: Christian Huitema
chat.text: Boo

chat.author: Christian Huitema
chat.text: (Boo to Jana)

chat.author: Christian Huitema
chat.text: +1 This is great work!

chat.author: Reese Enghardt
chat.text: Thanks all!
